   </instructions>   <bead id="ddx-1c3a78f2">     <title>agent: make missing bead-lifecycle hook diagnostics actionable</title>     <description> PROBLEM
When the post-attempt triage hook cannot find the nested `bead-lifecycle` skill, the operator sees `post-attempt triage error (<bead> execution_failed): triage hook: skill missing: bead-lifecycle (continuing)`. The message is technically true but does not say which project root was inspected, which paths were checked, or what command remediates the install.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go:90-92` returns `triage hook: skill missing: bead-lifecycle` when `hasBeadLifecycleSkill(projectRoot)` is false. `cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go:218-227` hard-codes the checked paths but returns only a boolean, so callers cannot include the checked path list in diagnostics. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:175-179` logs the hook error verbatim and appends `(continuing)`, which confirms fail-open behavior but hides the actionable remediation.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace the boolean-only missing-skill path with a diagnostic helper that returns whether the lifecycle skill exists, the resolved project root, and the exact checked paths.
- Update post-attempt triage errors to say the hook was skipped/degraded because `bead-lifecycle` was missing under the project root.
- Include the checked paths `.agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` and `.claude/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/SKILL.md` in the error text.
- Include remediation in the hook-time error: run `ddx update --force`, then `ddx doctor`.
- Preserve fail-open behavior: missing lifecycle skill must not change the underlying attempt status or close/park behavior by itself.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not run full `ddx doctor` from inside the hook.
- Do not dispatch the lifecycle hook through a fallback global skill path.
- Do not change lint/readiness/triage JSON output contracts.
- Do not change retry or cooldown policy in this bead.
